item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.3 No.15, 1955
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE No. 15 (51) Vol. III 1955
of a voyage round the world (v)
"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 2s 6d. A COPY
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £3. 3s. (9s or equivalent); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
SUBSCRIPTIONS FROM OUTSIDE THE STERLING AREA MAY BE PAID THROUGH THE UNESCO COUPON SCHEME
pages.handwritten_text:
Betty Davis
Joe Mankow [?]
Canasta with Marlon Dietrich
Lew [?]
Ride Great Comnik [?]
Shirley Temple 10 Q M
"Dear Mother, I've arrived Hollywood Port of Mx"
sketch_descriptions: A crowded film camera scene featuring a person behind a large movie camera with other individuals seated or standing nearby, some facing each other and engaged in conversation.
A group of several people playing canasta, including a clear profile of one person and a detailed figure of a woman with earrings, identified as Marlon Dietrich.
A busy outdoor street scene showing various people walking, a woman holding a tennis racket, and signboards with words like 'Cinegrill' and other advertising.
A smaller sketch depicting a woman and two men in hats, one bending over to write or sign something, with a background of a building labeled 'Montgomery'.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle 1955 features sketches related to a Hollywood visit, including scenes from a film studio, a card game with Marlon Dietrich, street scenes with pedestrians, and a note about arriving in Hollywood.

pages.typeset_text:
516 Work  517,522-525 Play  518,519 Fans  520 New arrival  521 THE newspaper columnist  526 Technicians
HOLLYWOOD
pages.handwritten_text: Losquilla Parsons the Communist  Fish lobster  Charles Boyer  Vincent Bennett  Gloria Swanson's party  John Kennedy  Adrian Neel  Billy Haley  Mr. Clark Collins (costume fitting)  Hedy Lamarr  HQ of Paramount  STAGE 2  STAGE 2, almost empty on bed lights  Film studio workers
sketch_descriptions: Top-left group: A sketch of a man labeled 'Losquilla Parsons the Communist' along with several people engaged in social conversation, including one identified as Charles Boyer and another as Vincent Bennett.
Top-right group: Several figures at what appears to be Gloria Swanson's party, including a woman wearing a hat and multiple others loosely sketched in conversation.
Bottom-left group: A social scene including John Kennedy, Adrian Neel, and Billy Haley, along with a man identified as Mr. Clark Collins in costume fitting, and Hedy Lamarr alongside a smiling man.
Bottom-right group: A backstage film studio scene showing several technicians or workers near an area labeled 'Stage 2,' with some figures facing a man in a cowboy hat.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle features a series of lively sketches depicting Hollywood social scenes, including notable personalities at Gloria Swanson's party, actors and technicians backstage at Stage 2, and other film-related moments.
